expected_gaps.category: Identity Management, Authentication and Access Control
expected_gaps.description: Identities and credentials are issued, managed, verified, revoked, and audited for authorized devices, users, and processes
expected_gaps.reason: Policy does not address identity lifecycle management or credential management processes
expected_gaps.evidence: No mention of identity provisioning, credential issuance, or access credential management

expected_gaps.category: Identity Management, Authentication and Access Control
expected_gaps.description: Identities are proofed and bound to credentials and asserted in interactions
expected_gaps.reason: Policy lacks identity proofing and credential binding requirements
expected_gaps.evidence: No identity verification or authentication credential binding described

expected_gaps.category: Identity Management, Authentication and Access Control
expected_gaps.description: Users, devices, and other assets are authenticated
expected_gaps.reason: Policy does not specify authentication requirements or mechanisms
expected_gaps.evidence: No authentication methods, multi-factor authentication, or device authentication mentioned

expected_gaps.category: Identity Management, Authentication and Access Control
expected_gaps.description: Identity assertions are protected, conveyed, and verified
expected_gaps.reason: Policy lacks requirements for protecting and verifying identity assertions
expected_gaps.evidence: No identity assertion protection or verification processes described

expected_gaps.category: Identity Management, Authentication and Access Control
expected_gaps.description: Access permissions, entitlements, and authorizations are defined in a policy, managed, enforced, and reviewed, and incorporate the principles of least privilege and separation of duties
expected_gaps.reason: Policy mentions access but does not detail authorization management or least privilege enforcement
expected_gaps.evidence: Section 8.1 mentions security measures generically but lacks specific access control implementation

expected_gaps.category: Identity Management, Authentication and Access Control
expected_gaps.description: Physical access to assets is managed and protected
expected_gaps.reason: Policy does not address physical access controls for data storage or processing facilities
expected_gaps.evidence: No physical security or facility access controls mentioned

expected_gaps.reason: Policy mentions encryption generically but lacks specific data-at-rest protection requirements
expected_gaps.evidence: Section 8.1 mentions 'encryption of data at rest' but provides no implementation details, key management, or encryption standards

expected_gaps.reason: Policy mentions encryption in transit but lacks specific protocols and requirements
expected_gaps.evidence: Section 8.1 mentions 'encryption of data in transit' but no TLS/SSL requirements, protocol versions, or cipher suites specified

expected_gaps.description: Protections against data leaks are implemented
expected_gaps.reason: Policy does not address data loss prevention or data leakage protection mechanisms
expected_gaps.evidence: No DLP, data exfiltration prevention, or leakage detection mentioned

expected_gaps.description: Integrity checking mechanisms are used to verify hardware integrity
expected_gaps.reason: Policy does not address hardware integrity verification or secure boot
expected_gaps.evidence: No hardware integrity, TPM, or secure boot requirements mentioned

expected_gaps.description: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-use are protected
expected_gaps.reason: Policy does not address protection of data during processing or in-memory
expected_gaps.evidence: No data-in-use protection, memory encryption, or secure enclaves mentioned

expected_gaps.description: Backups of data are created, protected, maintained, and tested
expected_gaps.reason: Policy does not address backup requirements, protection, or testing
expected_gaps.evidence: No backup strategy, backup encryption, or recovery testing mentioned

notes: The Data Privacy policy intentionally omits PR.AA (Identity Management and Access Control) implementation details
The policy also omits PR.DS (Data Security) technical controls and implementation requirements
While the policy mentions encryption and access controls at a high level, it lacks the specific technical requirements that NIST CSF expects
At least 10 out of 12 gaps (83.3%) must be detected to pass validation
Note: The policy correctly addresses data privacy principles but fails on cybersecurity technical controls
